About: It is about a variant of softball.
Affects: It affects the way fielders play by not using gloves or mitts.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with leagues that have existed since the 1960s in Portland, Oregon.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the category of softball variants.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a larger ball that gradually becomes softer the more the ball is hit.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by George Hancock.
DevelopedIn: It was developed in Chicago in the 19th century.
HasA: It has a larger ball than standard softball.
It has no gloves or mitts used by fielders.
It has higher arched pitching.
It has balls and strikes determined by where the ball lands and crosses the batter's body.
It has leagues that may form co-ed, all-male, or all-female teams.
